channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: Musk's X admits noncompliance with Australian child protection request
item.description: Elon ​Musk's social media company X Corp has ​admitted ‌to not complying with an order from Australia's eSafety commissioner to ⁠provide information about the measures it has in place ​to stop ‌child exploitation on the X platform.

media:group.media:description: Elon Musk's social media platform did not comply with an order from Australia's eSafety commissioner.

item.title: Breaking: Woman fighting for life after being hit by police car in Cairns
item.description: A woman is fighting for life after being hit by a police car in Cairns on Thursday morning.

media:group.media:description: A woman is fighting for life after being hit by a police vehicle in Cairns this morning.

item.title: Last private midwife north of Perth to close as costs of business rise
item.description: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Melville

media:group.media:description: Cath Tan says legislative changes are part of the reason she will pause her practise.

item.title: Prince William goes wild in crowd as Aston Villa ends 30-year drought
item.description: In front of a joyful future king of England, Aston Villa coach Unai Emery reaffirmed his status as the king of the Europa League as Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Istanbul to secure Emery a record-extending fifth title in the Europa League.

media:group.media:description: Prince William goes wild in the stands as Aston Villa score against Freiburg in the Europa League final.

item.title: What is broken heart syndrome and who is most at risk?
item.description: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: Experts say postmenopausal women are most at risk of broken heart syndrome.

item.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects
item.description: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.
item.dc:creator: Oliver Brown and Andrew Schmidt

media:group.media:description: Fourth generation grazier Sam Beven, with his son Duncan, is opening the gate to renewable energy on his outback station.

item.title: Trump's latest trophy proves his power over his party, despite the polls
item.description: Donald Trump's revenge tour is in full swing, and his latest victim is a Republican who has voted with his party more than 90 per cent of the time.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Day

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump's approval rating is sinking but he retains huge sway over his party.

item.title: Breaking: Bomb squad called as Melbourne's Avalon Airport evacuated
item.description: A person has been arrested after a suspicious item was found during security screening at the airport early this morning.

media:group.media:description: Avalon Airport was evacuated after a suspicious item was found during security screening.

item.title: Elderly woman dies 11 days after fatal crash on Queensland's Southern Downs
item.description: The woman has died of injuries sustained as a passenger in an SUV that allegedly swerved to avoid an oncoming car in the wrong lane on May 9.
item.dc:creator: Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Police say the 85-year-old woman was a passenger in a vehicle that swerved to avoid oncoming traffic.

item.title: Russian jet fighters dangerously intercept RAF plane over Black Sea
item.description: Two Russian jets have intercepted a Royal Air Force plane over the Black Sea, flying close enough to trigger its emergency systems.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: A Russian Su-27, dangerously intercepted an RAF Rivet Joint aircraft (not pictured) according to the UK Ministry of Defence.

item.title: Whitsunday tour operators fear e-bike ban could destroy business
item.description: Guided tour operators say proposed laws banning under-16s from using personal mobility devices will put them out of businesses.
item.dc:creator: Bryn Wakefield

media:group.media:description: Dylan Ford says his business could not operate with normal bikes.

item.title: NRL star Arrow reveals MND has already affected his speech
item.description: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

media:group.media:description: Jai Arrow faces the media during a press conference to announce his retirement from the NRL.

item.title: As coral reefs are wiped out from bleaching, this one brings hope
item.description: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.
item.dc:creator: Piper Duffy

media:group.media:description: A coral reef in the Abrolhos Islands, off the coast of Geraldton, withstood exposure to extreme heat, researchers have found.

item.title: Why 70,000 people could decide future for 70 million Brits
item.description: Andy Burnham is angling to become the UK's next prime minister. He has plenty of popularity, but no seat in parliament. That could be about to change.
item.dc:creator: Mazoe Ford

media:group.media:description: Andy Burnham is a major force in the UK's Labour Party.

item.title: Delays could see nation's first offshore wind project blown off course
item.description: Environmental timelines outlined by Star of the South show the offshore wind farm project could take up to an additional five years to be completed, leaving some in the energy sector wondering whether Victoria could suffer an energy shortfall.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope

media:group.media:description: Star of the South is planned to begin construction in 2030.

item.title: Congo cancels World Cup farewell as US clarifies Ebola quarantine rules
item.description: The Congo football team cancels its farewell in Kinshasa as the US clarified what the rules would be for players and staff entering the country for the World Cup.

media:group.media:description: Fans from Congo will face strict quarantine rules ahead of the World Cup in the US.

item.title: Gen Zs who approach share market like 'lottery ticket' could pay more tax
item.description: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Young investors who take bigger risks will pay more tax under the government's proposed changes to CGT.

item.title: Live: ASIC lodges more First Guardian legal action, alleging 'diligence' failures by Equity Trustees
item.description: The corporate watchdog alleges ASX-listed Equity Trustees did not obtain critical information before onboarding First Guardian. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: The ASX has hit a series of highs in the past two weeks, raising questions about an apparently growing investor appetite for risk.

item.title: Iran examining US proposal as Trump says talks in 'final stages'
item.description: Iran says it is examining the latest US proposal received from mediator Pakistan in talks to end the Middle East war, despite the two sides trading threats over resuming attacks.

media:group.media:description: While the ceasefire has halted fighting, it has not reopened the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback
item.description: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: Amid Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak in decades, public health messaging in the NT has been hard to find.

item.title: Live: Paul Keating backs CGT changes as 'structurally sound'
item.description: Former prime minister Paul Keating says the government's changes to capital gains tax are "structurally sound" and seek to "arrest" the growth in house prices. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Former prime minister Paul Keating speaks to the media during a press conference at the Barangaroo precinct, in Sydney on October 20, 2022.

item.title: Hackers in an 'ethical battle' beyond cyber attacks and exploits
item.description: Student hackers compete in the International Cybersecurity Challenge 2026, an ethical hacking contest simulating real cyber attacks and defences with real-world consequences.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Cansdale

media:group.media:description: Rohan va Klinken from Team Oceania at the 2026 Cybersecurity Challenge.

item.title: How watching Aussies win Olympic gold 'changed everything' for rugby star
item.description: When Isabella Nasser tuned in to watch the 2016 Rio Olympics as a 13-year-old, she had little idea of the pathway her life was about to take.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australian co-captain Isabella Nasser was inspired to play rugby sevens as a teenager.

item.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask
item.description: ACT industry groups say drug use and widespread construction are among issues eroding feelings of safety.
item.dc:creator: Lily Nothling

media:group.media:description: Canberrans have mixed perceptions when it comes to safety in Civic with some fearful of anti-social behaviour.

item.title: 4yo child healing after family violence thanks to 'guardian angel'
item.description: When Claire arrived at a women's refuge in outer Sydney with her four-year-old daughter in tow, the child was "barely able to speak clearly".
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross

media:group.media:description: Claire is thankful for the support of a "guardian angel" caseworker for helping her child.
